mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike sequences with t-stat > 1.96, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in > 55% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand that depresses corporate revenue forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative 60-day returns when either series reverts to growth within two months, or out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term market repricing, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive during industrial production contractions when the federal funds rate is below 3.0%, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or increasing federal funds rate creates a real rate squeeze that erodes equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 60% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of months with payroll growth exceeding 100000, or the correlation coefficient between payroll changes and returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate falling below 1.5% signals severe economic deceleration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The market produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of low-GDP-release instances, or the signal fails to predict returns when GDP revisions are positive.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production alongside stable unemployment reflects healthy capacity utilization that boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns turn negative when industrial production growth exceeds 2.0% while unemployment remains flat, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in backtests.
